description.value:
Max Ranks: 5

A Bleeding creature has suffered a minor abrasion to their flesh, causing slow blood loss if untreated.

  • At the start of their turn, a bleeding creature takes 1d4 necrotic damage for each rank of Bleed they have.

  • A Bleeding creature cannot gain the benefits of a rest.

  • As an Action, a Bleeding creature may attempt to stop the bleeding by succeeding on a DC 15 Medicine check.

  • A creature who would gain a sixth (or more) ranks of the Bleeding condition immediately gains 5 ranks of the Lacerated condition, ending the Bleeding condition.

    • The first time per rest a Bleeding creature would gain the Lacerated this way, they must succeed a DC 11 flat check, immediately taking the damage of their Lacerated ranks as they suffer a great wound.

  • Outside of combat, every 10-minutes a creature suffers the effects of this condition as if a turn has passed in combat.
